rag_rules: Insert AGREE annex blocks into the intended target component or implementation while preserving AADL architecture
Place assumptions and guarantees in valid component type scopes
Place implementation-level bindings in valid implementation scopes
Use only visible identifiers from the current scope
Preserve the authoritative source of component names, ports, directions, data types, subcomponents, and connections from the input AADL model
generic_rules: Use case-provided thresholds and legal visible feature names in contracts
Align helper declaration types with the expression types
Do not encode AADL timing properties as AGREE expressions unless the model exposes explicit typed ports or constants for them
Use exact Boolean expressions only when the port is an AGREE bool in scope; otherwise omit the unsafe clause
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are written inside `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks
Use `eq` for equality comparisons and `and` for logical conjunctions
Use `value_in >= lower_limit and value_in <= upper_limit` for range checks
anti_patterns: Do not use implementation-only subcomponent paths in a component type annex
Do not use subcomponent ports in a component type contract unless represented through valid variables or outputs
Do not convert Boolean domain values into integers for range comparisons
irrelevant_topics: AGREE, annex, assume, guarantee, assign, validator, or repair
